description: The sugar palm, scientifically known as Borassus flabellifer, is a tropical palm tree native to South and Southeast Asia. It is widely cultivated for its sweet sap, which can be processed into sugar, syrup, and alcoholic beverages. The tree can grow up to 30 meters tall and has large, fan-shaped leaves. The sugar palm is an important economic resource in many regions, providing food, fuel, and materials for construction and handicrafts.
